creator: mikeg@bsd-box.net
text:
I agree this shouldn't cause core dumps -- Having the conf file unreadable
is a misconfigration and should be reported, but an assert() / bail-out
isn't helpful, and is probably too strong here since as you noticed it can
cause stuff to dump core.

I'll look at this in a few days (my dev environment is currently being
rebuilt) and see about implementing more graceful handling & a meaningful
log message.  My inclination is to get this fixed in the upcoming nss_ldap
266 release, but adding a fix in the port shouldn't be much trouble.


In the interim your obvious fix is the correct workaround: Make sure the
config file is world-readable :-)

-MG
